'''James Laithwaite''' (born 23 September 1991) is an  English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played for the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and the [[Bradford Bulls]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]].<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

He previously played for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]].

==Club career==
Laithwaite started his career at [[Warrington Wolves]]. He made his Super League début [[Loan (sports)|on loan]] with [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] during 2012. In 2012 he was also [[Super League#Dual registration|dual registered]], and played for [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]].<ref name="Back rower James Laithwaite signs for Leigh Centurions">{{cite web|url=http://www.leighjournal.co.uk/sport/leighcenturions/9621686.Back_rower_James_Laithwaite_signs_for_Leigh_Centurions|title=Back rower James Laithwaite signs for Leigh Centurions|publisher=leighjournal.co.uk|date=29 March 2012|accessdate=1 January 2013}}</ref> He eventually made his début for Warrington against Hull KR in March 2013.<ref name="Warrington's James Laithwaite joins Hull KR on loan">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/18664762|title=Warrington's James Laithwaite joins Hull KR on loan|publisher=bbc.co.uk|date=1 July 2012|access-date=1 January 2013}}</ref> A similar dual registration with [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]] for the 2013 season followed during which Laithwaite broke his ankle playing against [[Featherstone Rovers]] on 1 April 2013.<ref name="Warrington lose James Laithwaite for six weeks after forward breaks ankle">{{cite web|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12209/8613095/Warrington-lose-James-Laithwaite-for-six-weeks-after-forward-breaks-ankle|title=Warrington lose James Laithwaite for six weeks after forward breaks ankle|publisher=skysports.com|date=2 April 2013|accessdate=1 January 2014}}</ref>

Laithwaite suffered a broken leg in June 2015 which ruled him out for the 2016 Super League season. In July 2016 it was announced that he would be joining [[Toronto Wolfpack]] for the 2017 season.

In 2018 he was dual registered with the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[2018 RFL League 1]] where he made 12 appearances and scored 4 tries, however his season was cut short when he suffered a broken leg in the 36–16 victory over [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.]] Following this, Laithwaite announced his retirement from rugby league at the age of 27 to focus on his business career.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/17189939.bradford-bulls-kear-says-new-boy-farrell-can-replicate-laithwaite/|title=Bradford Bulls: Kear says new boy Farrell can replicate Laithwaite|publisher=Telegraph & Argus|date=31 October 2018|accessdate=6 December 2018}}</ref>

==Retirement==
In 2019, at the age of 27, after suffering a fourth leg break during his time at Bradford, he decided to retire; and has gone into the healthy food business with his partner, Michelle McDowall.<ref>{{cite web|website=Warrington Guardian|date=5 Sep 2019|accessdate=14 May 2021|title=Ex-Warrington Wolves forward James Laithwaite opens Fuel Hub|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/news/17878321.ex-warrington-wolves-forward-james-laithwaite-opens-fuel-hub/}}</ref>
